hints.text: Swedish: **Å Ä Ö** (Danish/Norwegian have only Å; Finnish has Ä Ö + long double-vowel words)
**All road lines white** (Norway & Finland use yellow); highway outer line split into sectioned pattern
Road signs = red border + **yellow background**; **chevrons = yellow arrows on blue** (only Sweden in Europe)
Lots of **red+yellow** infrastructure (roadwork, signpost stripes) — distinctive to Sweden
Bollards black+white (orange reflector at intersections); wooden poles w/ small **black cap on top** (Nordic)
Pedestrian sign **4 stripes** (Finland/Denmark = 5); blue signs with a large white **M** on small roads
Street suffixes **vägen / gatan** (v / g); dark-red painted wooden countryside houses (Nordic)
**Volvo ≈ 30% of cars**; Swedish church flag (yellow, red cross, crown) near churches
Gen4 cars: red / white / blue (only the blue one appears in NO/FI/DK); Gen3 white car long antenna
North & west = mountainous w/ long narrow lakes; south & east = flatter; boreal forest + many lakes
Snow poles orange w/ reflector above middle (Finland's is thinner, near the top)
